'''Chris Patolo''' (born 23 July 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer nicknamed ''The Postman'' who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] & [[Manly Sea Eagles]]

A local junior from the Bankstown Sports club, he was a member of Canterbury's Harold Matthews and SG Ball sides before progressing through the club’s High-Performance Youth Program and Jersey Flegg side.

==Playing career==

===2021===
In round 16 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Patolo made his debut for [[Canterbury Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/03/saab-and-turbo-hit-top-gear-as-sea-eagles-cruise-rout-66/|title=Saab and Turbo hit top gear as Sea Eagles cruise Rout 66|website=www.nrl.com|date=3 July 2021}}</ref>
Patolo made a total of six appearances for Canterbury in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished last and claimed the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/canterbury-bulldogs-set-to-sign-max-king-and-braidon-burns/a9fa54c9-a224-4cc9-ae15-8a5dd1e74275/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs set to sign Rabbitohs star Braidon Burns, Storm prop Max King for 2022 NRL season|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2022===
Patolo played a total of 15 matches for Canterbury in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/wapmyddrbadukioadiybr1fs/|title=NRL 2022: Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=12 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
Patolo only played two matches for Canterbury in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/ubsemk2vg6ihykdbrn33revb#:~:text=Did%20the%20Bulldogs%20over%2Ddeliver,their%20roster%20and%20coaching%20staff./|title=NRL 2023: Canterbury Bulldogs season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2024===
Patolo managed just 4 matches for Canterbury in the [[2024 NRL season]], primarily playing in reserve grade as the club finished 6th on the table.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-09-16 |title='Frustrated' reality laid bare from stunning Bulldogs rise |url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review-phil-gould-cameron-ciraldo-20240916-p5kb0n.html |access-date=2024-11-15 |website=Nine |language=en}}</ref>

On 19 October, Patolo would sign a train-and-trial contract with the Manly Sea Eagles.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-10-19 |title=Journeyman Chris Patolo joins Manly on a train-and-trial deal |url=https://www.rugby-addict.com/en-au/article/bulldogs/journeyman-chris-patolo-joins-manly-on-a-train-and-trial-deal/67134fb96f60f849c1030d63 |access-date=2024-10-19 |website=www.rugby-addict.com |language=en-au}}</ref>

===2025===
On 9 October 2025 it was reported that he signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] for 2026 on a 2-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Huddersfield Giants swoop to sign forward from Down Under as length of contract confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-swoop-to-sign-chris-patolo}}</ref>
